Your Village API token. Generate one fro: Your Village API token. Generate one from your Village settings and paste it here.
Get information about the partner applic: Get information about the partner application associated with your token — title, description, domain, logo URL, public key, and active flag. Useful for verifying your app configuration.
Quickly check if you have connections to: Quickly check if you have connections to a company without fetching full details. Returns whether paths exist, an overall score, count of reachable people, and avatar thumbnails.
Refresh/import company data from LinkedI: Refresh/import company data from LinkedIn URLs or domains. Realtime mode returns enriched data synchronously (or times out after 25s); async mode returns job IDs for later status checking. At least one of linkedin_urls or domains must be provided.
Get detailed information about a company: Get detailed information about a company by domain, LinkedIn URL, or any URL. Returns name, website, LinkedIn URL, logo, employee count, industry, founded year, and description.
Get detailed information for up to 10 co: Get detailed information for up to 10 companies in a single request. Each result contains either company data or an error.
Find introduction paths to people at a s: Find introduction paths to people at a specific company. Returns up to 50 employees you can reach through your network, ranked by connection strength.
Get your top-connected companies ranked: Get your top-connected companies ranked by network strength. Returns a paginated list with connection score, label, LinkedIn URL, domain, and enrichment data.
Search for companies using natural langu: Search for companies using natural language (in the "prompt" field) and/or structured filters. Returns matching companies with relevance and connection-strength scores; supports cursor-based pagination.
Rank a list of companies (up to 100 Link: Rank a list of companies (up to 100 LinkedIn URLs or domains) by how well-connected you are to people there. Each result includes a score, label, LinkedIn URL, and domain.
Join a group using an invite link. Once: Join a group using an invite link. Once joined, you gain access to the shared network of all group members.
Leave a group you are a member of. If yo: Leave a group you are a member of. If you are the last member, the group is deleted. Admins must remove all other members before leaving.
Get all groups (communities) you are a m: Get all groups (communities) you are a member of. Groups allow members to share network access with each other, expanding everyone's reach.
Create a new group or update an existing: Create a new group or update an existing one. To create: provide a name (id is auto-generated). To update: provide group_id (requires admin permissions).
Get all your connected Gmail integration: Get all your connected Gmail integrations, including rate limits, timezone, the default sending account, and whether you can add more based on your plan.
Trigger a full resync of a Google or Lin: Trigger a full resync of a Google or LinkedIn integration. Clears sync cursors and re-imports all contacts/calendar data; all graph operations use MERGE so it is fully idempotent.
Update settings for a connected Gmail in: Update settings for a connected Gmail integration — adjust rate limits, timezone, status, or set as the default sending account. Provide only the fields you want to change.
Add people or companies to a list. Each: Add people or companies to a list. Each identifier is resolved (LinkedIn URLs, company domains, emails, or graph IDs) and added. Returns detailed results per item.
Check which lists contain the given enti: Check which lists contain the given entities. Returns a map of entity_id to the lists that contain it. Entities not in any list are omitted.
Create a new list to organize people or: Create a new list to organize people or companies. The creator becomes the list owner. List creation may be limited based on your subscription plan.
Permanently delete a list and all its it: Permanently delete a list and all its items. This action cannot be undone.
Get a list with all its items. Returns t: Get a list with all its items. Returns the list metadata plus paginated items. For people lists, items are sorted by connection strength (warmth score).
Get all your saved lists of people or co: Get all your saved lists of people or companies. Supports pagination, filtering by type, and searching by title.
Remove items from a list by their item I: Remove items from a list by their item IDs (obtained from get_list). Returns the count of successfully removed items.
Update a list's metadata. Provide at lea: Update a list's metadata. Provide at least one of: title or description. The list type cannot be changed after creation.
Get detailed profile information (name,: Get detailed profile information (name, headline, current company, location, photo, LinkedIn URL) for a person identified by LinkedIn URL or generic URL.
Get detailed profile information for up: Get detailed profile information for up to 10 people in a single request. Partial results are returned if some lookups fail — check each result for success or error status.
Find the email address for a person by L: Find the email address for a person by LinkedIn URL or generic URL. Returns the verified email address if found, or null if unavailable.
Find email addresses for up to 10 people: Find email addresses for up to 10 people in a single request. Returns email addresses where found, with error messages for failed lookups.
Find introduction paths to reach a speci: Find introduction paths to reach a specific person through your professional network. Provide a LinkedIn URL and get back direct connections, mutual contacts, and connection strength scores (0-100).
Find introduction paths to multiple peop: Find introduction paths to multiple people in a single request. Provide up to 100 LinkedIn IDs (the slug after linkedin.com/in/) and get connection paths for each.
Get your network contacts ranked by conn: Get your network contacts ranked by connection strength. Returns a paginated list with enrichment data, supporting cursor-based pagination and optional connection-degree filtering.
Refresh or import people data from Linke: Refresh or import people data from LinkedIn URLs. In realtime mode, returns enriched person data immediately (or times out after 25 seconds and converts to async). In async mode, returns job IDs for later status checking.
Search for people using natural language: Search for people using natural language queries. Pass your search text in "prompt" (e.g. "engineers at Google"). Supports cursor-based pagination and structured filters.
Rank a list of LinkedIn profile URLs by: Rank a list of LinkedIn profile URLs by how well-connected you are to them. Returns each person sorted by connection strength (highest first), with score (0-100) and score_label.
Join a team using an invite link. Provid: Join a team using an invite link. Provide the invite link code (the part after /join/ in the URL).
Leave a team you are a member of. If you: Leave a team you are a member of. If you are the last member of a non-paid team, the team is deleted. Admins of paid teams cannot leave — use Cancel Plan instead.
Get all teams you are a member of. Each: Get all teams you are a member of. Each team includes id, name, logo, invite_link, and an is_admin flag indicating your admin status.
Create or update a team. The team ID is: Create or update a team. The team ID is derived from the team name; repeated calls with the same name update the same team (requires admin permissions on update).
Get profile information for the authenti: Get profile information for the authenticated user — id, email, name, account creation date, sync completion status, and active flag. Useful for verifying auth and checking whether the user's network data is ready.
Import manual relationships to expand yo: Import manual relationships to expand your network graph — useful for adding people you know but aren't connected to digitally. Provide email addresses or LinkedIn URLs with optional scores (0-10); higher scores indicate stronger relationships. Max 1000 per request.
Name: Name
Title: Title
Company domain, e.g. \"acme.com\" (provi: Company domain, e.g. "acme.com" (provide one of: domain, linkedin_url, url)
LinkedIn company page URL, e.g. \"https:: LinkedIn company page URL, e.g. "https://linkedin.com/company/acme-corp" (provide one of: domain, linkedin_url, url)
Generic company URL (auto-detected) (pro: Generic company URL (auto-detected) (provide one of: domain, linkedin_url, url)
Job IDs to check status for (returned by: Job IDs to check status for (returned by async refresh calls).
LinkedIn company page URLs to refresh.
If true, wait for enrichment (up to ~25s: If true, wait for enrichment (up to ~25s) and return company data inline. If false, return job IDs immediately.
Company domain, e.g. \"example.com\" (pr: Company domain, e.g. "example.com" (provide one of: domain, linkedin_url, url)
Up to 10 companies to look up. Each row: Up to 10 companies to look up. Each row specifies an identifier type and value.
Opaque cursor for pagination (from previ: Opaque cursor for pagination (from previous response)
Number of results per page (default 25,: Number of results per page (default 25, max 50)
Filter by connection degree (1=direct, 2: Filter by connection degree (1=direct, 2=second-degree, 3=third-degree). Provide one or more values.
Number of results per page (default 25,: Number of results per page (default 25, max 100)
Natural language search query, e.g. \"Fi: Natural language search query, e.g. "Find B2B SaaS companies with 50-200 employees in California". Either prompt or filters must be provided.
Structured filters. Supported keys: name: Structured filters. Supported keys: name, domain, linkedinUrl, linkedinId, industry, companySize, companyType, country, city, foundedAfter, foundedBefore, keywords.
Maximum number of results per page (defa: Maximum number of results per page (default 25, max 100)
Search mode: auto (let system decide), l: Search mode: auto (let system decide), lookup (direct match), simple (LLM-powered), or complex (thinking + high-reasoning SQL generation).
Search scope: network (connections first: Search scope: network (connections first) or global (all companies).
Pagination cursor (encoded string) from: Pagination cursor (encoded string) from a previous response.
Inline count scope to compute on the fir: Inline count scope to compute on the first page only. Ignored when cursor is provided and adds latency.
Array of company URLs (LinkedIn URLs or: Array of company URLs (LinkedIn URLs or domains), e.g. "https://linkedin.com/company/acme-corp" or "example.com". Max 100.
The invite link code shared by a group a: The invite link code shared by a group admin or member
ID of the group to leave: ID of the group to leave
Existing group ID to update. Omit to cre: Existing group ID to update. Omit to create a new group.
URL to the group logo image: URL to the group logo image
Numeric ID of the integration to resync: Numeric ID of the integration to resync
Numeric ID of the integration to update: Numeric ID of the integration to update
Minimum delay between emails in seconds: Minimum delay between emails in seconds (60-3600)
Whether this should be the default integ: Whether this should be the default integration for email sending
Items to add to the list (max 100): Items to add to the list (max 100)
Array of entity IDs (person or company I: Array of entity IDs (person or company IDs) to check membership for (max 1000)
Type of entities in the list: Type of entities in the list
Whether the list is publicly accessible: Whether the list is publicly accessible
Opaque cursor for items pagination (from: Opaque cursor for items pagination (from previous response)
Array of item IDs to remove from the lis: Array of item IDs to remove from the list (max 100)
New list description. Leave blank to kee: New list description. Leave blank to keep unchanged.
LinkedIn profile URL, e.g. https://linke: LinkedIn profile URL, e.g. https://linkedin.com/in/johndoe (provide this OR url)
Generic URL — auto-detected (provide thi: Generic URL — auto-detected (provide this OR linkedin_url)
Up to 10 people to enrich. Each row is o: Up to 10 people to enrich. Each row is one person.
Up to 10 people to look up emails for. E: Up to 10 people to look up emails for. Each row is one person.
LinkedIn profile URL of the target perso: LinkedIn profile URL of the target person, e.g. https://linkedin.com/in/johndoe (provide this OR url)
LinkedIn identifiers (e.g. \"johndoe\" f: LinkedIn identifiers (e.g. "johndoe" from linkedin.com/in/johndoe). Max 100 per request.
Filter by current company domain (exact: Filter by current company domain (exact match), e.g. "google.com"
LinkedIn profile URLs to refresh. At lea: LinkedIn profile URLs to refresh. At least one is required.
If true, waits synchronously for results: If true, waits synchronously for results (up to 25s). If false, returns job IDs for async processing.
Natural language search query (e.g. \"Fi: Natural language search query (e.g. "Find CTOs at SaaS companies in San Francisco")
Structured search filters as JSON (alter: Structured search filters as JSON (alternative or complement to prompt). Either prompt or filters must be provided.
Maximum number of results per page (1-10: Maximum number of results per page (1-100, default 25)
Search execution mode. \"auto\" classifi: Search execution mode. "auto" classifies the query. Use "lookup" for direct matches, "simple" for firmographic queries, and "complex" for multi-step queries.
\"network\" searches your connections fi: "network" searches your connections first, "global" searches everyone, "specific_list" restricts to caller-provided identifiers (requires specific_list_data).
Pagination cursor (encoded string from a: Pagination cursor (encoded string from a previous response)
Inline count scopes to compute on the fi: Inline count scopes to compute on the first page only. Ignored when cursor is provided and adds latency.
Required when scope=\"specific_list\". C: Required when scope="specific_list". Caller-supplied candidate set (1..50,000 entries). Each entry is a bare LinkedIn identifier or { id, score? }.
Array of LinkedIn person profile URLs (e: Array of LinkedIn person profile URLs (e.g. https://linkedin.com/in/johndoe). At least one is required.
The invite link code shared by a team ad: The invite link code shared by a team admin (e.g. the part after /join/ in the invite URL)
ID of the team to leave: ID of the team to leave
URL to the team logo image: URL to the team logo image
Up to 1000 people to import. Each row: i: Up to 1000 people to import. Each row: identifier (email or LinkedIn URL), optional score (0-10), optional relationship type.
Default score (0-10) applied to rows wit: Default score (0-10) applied to rows without an explicit score.
